psvr вҖ” Percentage-error Support Vector Regression

Package purpose

psvr is an R package implementing four SVR models derived from a unified mathematical framework for percentage-error loss functions. It targets forecasting contexts where targets yk > 0 are strictly positive and relative accuracy matters more than absolute accuracy.

Source paper: A Unified Family of Percentage-Error Support Vector Regression Models with Symmetric Kernel Extensions (MDPI Mathematics, 2026). Full derivations are in paper.tex at the repository root.

Mathematical notation

Symbol Meaning
N number of training samples
xk вҲҲ Rбө– input vector for sample k
yk > 0 strictly positive target (Assumption 2 in paper)
K(xi, xj) kernel function
C > 0 regularization parameter (Оө-SVR models)
Оө вүҘ 0 insensitivity tube width (Оө-SVR models)
О“ > 0 regularization parameter (LS-SVR models)
a вҲҲ {-1, 1} symmetry type: 1 = even, -1 = odd
ОІk = Оұk - Оұk* dual variable differences (Оө-SVR)
Оұk вҲҲ R dual / Lagrange multipliers (LS-SVR)
b bias term
О© вҲҲ R^{NГ—N} kernel matrix, О©kl = K(xk, xl)
О©s = ВҪ(О© + aО©*) symmetrized kernel matrix
О©*kl = K(xk, -xl) cross-negation kernel matrix
Ks(xi,xj) = K(xi,xj) + aВ·K(xi,-xj) symmetric kernel
YО“ = diag(y1ВІ/О“, вҖҰ, yNВІ/О“) target-weighted regularization diagonal

The four models

Model 1 вҖ” Оө-SVR with MAPE (Theorem 1 / Appendix A.1)

Primal:

min  ВҪвҖ–ПүвҖ–ВІ + C ОЈk (Оҫk + Оҫk*)
s.t. (100/yk)(yk - ПүбөҖПҶ(xk) - b) вүӨ Оө + Оҫk
     (100/yk)(ПүбөҖПҶ(xk) + b - yk) вүӨ Оө + Оҫk*
     Оҫk, Оҫk* вүҘ 0

Dual QP (max form):

max  -ВҪ ОЈk,l ОІk ОІl K(xk,xl) + ОЈk ОІk yk - (Оө/100) ОЈk |ОІk| yk
s.t. ОЈk ОІk = 0
     |ОІk| вүӨ 100C / yk    (sample-dependent box; yk small вҶ’ tight bound)

where ОІk = Оұk - Оұk*, |ОІk| = Оұk + Оұk*.

KKT: Пү = ОЈk ОІk ПҶ(xk), ОЈk ОІk = 0, 0 вүӨ Оұk, Оұk* вүӨ 100C/yk.

Prediction: f(x) = ОЈk ОІk K(xk, x) + b

Implementation: QP via osqp. Key files: R/mape_svr.R.

Model 2 вҖ” Symmetric Оө-SVR with MAPE (Theorem 2 / Appendix A.2)

Same primal as Model 1, augmented with symmetry constraint: ПүбөҖПҶ(xk) = a В· ПүбөҖПҶ(-xk)

Dual QP (max form):

max  -Вј ОЈk,l ОІk ОІl Ks(xk,xl) + ОЈk ОІk yk - (Оө/100) ОЈk |ОІk| yk
s.t. ОЈk ОІk = 0
     |ОІk| вүӨ 100C / yk    (unchanged from Model 1)

where Ks(xi,xj) = K(xi,xj) + aВ·K(xi,-xj).

The factor Вј (vs ВҪ in Model 1) arises from the symmetrization in the representer theorem (Appendix A.2): ПүбөҖПҶ(xk) = ВҪ ОЈl ОІl Ks(xl, xk), so the quadratic term picks up an extra ВҪ.

Prediction: f(x) = ВҪ ОЈk ОІk Ks(xk, x) + b

Implementation: QP via osqp. Build Ks = О© + aВ·О©* inline (no ВҪ), then pass P = ВјВ·Ks as the QP quadratic matrix вҖ” the Вј factor is absorbed into P directly. Do not use sym_kernel_matrix() here; that helper returns ВҪ(О© + aВ·О©*) = О©s, which is only correct for Model 4. Key files: R/mape_sym_svr.R.

Model 3 вҖ” LS-SVR with RMSPE (Theorem 3 / Appendix A.3)

Primal:

min  ВҪвҖ–ПүвҖ–ВІ + (О“/2) ОЈk ekВІ/ykВІ
s.t. yk = ПүбөҖПҶ(xk) + b + ek

KKT stationarity: - Пү = ОЈk Оұk ПҶ(xk) - ОЈk Оұk = 0 - ek = (ykВІ/О“) Оұk

Linear system (solve directly):

[ 0    1бөҖ      ] [b]   [0]
[ 1    О© + YО“  ] [Оұ] = [y]

where YО“ = diag(y1ВІ/О“, вҖҰ, yNВІ/О“) вҖ” added to the diagonal of О© only.

Prediction: f(x) = ОЈk Оұk K(xk, x) + b

Implementation: base::solve() on (N+1)Г—(N+1) augmented matrix. Key files: R/rmspe_lssvr.R.

Model 4 вҖ” Symmetric LS-SVR with RMSPE (Theorem 4 / Appendix A.4)

Same primal as Model 3, augmented with symmetry constraint.

Linear system:

[ 0    1бөҖ       ] [b]   [0]
[ 1    О©s + YО“  ] [Оұ] = [y]

where О©s = ВҪ(О© + aО©*), О©*kl = K(xk, -xl).

Symmetric representer: ПүбөҖПҶ(xk) = ОЈl (О©s)kl Оұl

Prediction: f(x) = ВҪ ОЈk Оұk Ks(xk, x) + b equivalently: f(x) = ОЈk Оұk [K(xk,x) + aВ·K(xk,-x)] / 2 + b

Implementation: base::solve() with О©s replacing О©. Key files: R/rmspe_sym_lssvr.R.

Kernel symmetry (Assumption 3 in paper)

Required for Models 2 and 4: - K(-xi, xj) = K(xi, -xj) - K(-xi, -xj) = K(xi, xj)

RBF kernel K(xi, xj) = exp(-вҖ–xi - xjвҖ–ВІ/2ПғВІ) satisfies both conditions: - K(-xi, -xj) = exp(-вҖ–-xi-(-xj)вҖ–ВІ/2ПғВІ) = exp(-вҖ–xi-xjвҖ–ВІ/2ПғВІ) = K(xi,xj) вң“ - K(-xi, xj) = exp(-вҖ–-xi-xjвҖ–ВІ/2ПғВІ) = exp(-вҖ–xi+xjвҖ–ВІ/2ПғВІ) = K(xi,-xj) вң“

Polynomial kernels of even degree also satisfy Assumption 3.

Implementation decisions

  • QP solver: osqp package (Models 1 & 2). Supports sparse matrices, no commercial license.
  • Linear solver: base::solve() (Models 3 & 4). Dense (N+1)Г—(N+1) system, no extra dep.
  • Target validation: all fit() functions call stopifnot(all(y > 0)).
  • Kernel interface: make_kernel(type, ...) returns a closure function(xi, xj) from type вҲҲ {"rbf", "linear", "polynomial"}. Shared across all four models.
  • YО“ storage: stored as a vector y^2 / gamma; added to diagonal via diag(Omega) <- diag(Omega) + y_gamma вҖ” never materializes a full NГ—N diagonal matrix.
  • S3 classes: each model returns a named list with class:
    • "psvr_mape" (Model 1)
    • "psvr_mape_sym" (Model 2)
    • "psvr_rmspe" (Model 3)
    • "psvr_rmspe_sym" (Model 4)
  • S3 methods: predict.psvr_*() for each class.
  • Documentation: roxygen2 with @param, @return, @examples.
  • Style: tidyverse / base R style: snake_case, <- assignment.
  • License: MIT.

Implementation order

R environment

  • R 4.5.3 at C:\Program Files\R\R-4.5.3\bin\Rscript.exe
  • Run from PowerShell: & "C:\Program Files\R\R-4.5.3\bin\Rscript.exe" script.R
  • Always use forward slashes or single-quoted strings in PowerShell to avoid \U Unicode escape errors
  • osqp 1.0.0 uses solve_osqp(P, q, A, l, u, pars) directly вҖ” not osqp(...)$solve() (S7 object, old R6 API is gone)
  • Load package in R session: devtools::load_all("C:/Users/behep/OneDrive - ITESO/PhD/00-Tesis/psvr")

Invariants to enforce everywhere

  • yk > 0 always вҖ” validated at fit time, never silently coerced.
  • a вҲҲ {-1, 1} for symmetric models.
  • The symmetric kernel Ks(xi, xj) = K(xi, xj) + aВ·K(xi, -xj) uses negation of xj, so the kernel closure must accept negative inputs even when training data is positive.
  • YО“ is diagonal вҖ” add to diag(Omega) in place, never build an NГ—N diagonal matrix.
  • Box constraints in Models 1 & 2 are per-sample: |ОІk| вүӨ 100C/yk.
  • Prediction for Model 2: multiply by ВҪ (from symmetric representer theorem).
  • Prediction for Model 4: use ВҪ(K(xk,x) + aВ·K(xk,-x)) per support vector.

On this page

Developed by Pablo Benavides-Herrera.
